python.code:
import requests

response = requests.get('https://api.example.com/users')
data = response.json()
print(data)

comparisons.explanation: Same concept: send a GET request, receive JSON data. JavaScript uses fetch() (built-in), Python uses the requests library. Both return a response object you parse with .json(). The HTTP protocol is the same regardless of language.

keyInsight: HTTP is a PROTOCOL — a set of rules independent of any programming language. Whether you use JavaScript, Python, Go, or Java, the request/response pattern, status codes, and methods are IDENTICAL. The only difference is the syntax for making the call in each language.
